[Fossil scientists think the first members of the Equida (which includes Horses, Zebras, and Donkeys) lived 55 million years ago—after dinosaurs & before people.] The types of horses we know of today have been around for more than 4 million years and first lived in North America. Around 6,000 years ago people near Kazakhstan worked with horses--using them to carry heavy loads, help farmers, and travel.
